steps.title: Determine residency eligibility
steps.description: Confirm whether either spouse meets New York residency requirements for filing.
steps.relevance: Residency rules affect which court may hear the case.

steps.title: Identify the type of divorce
steps.description: Determine whether the matter is likely uncontested or contested.
steps.relevance: Agreement on major issues often affects the forms and process path.

steps.title: Gather basic family information
steps.description: Information regarding children under 21 may affect custody, support, and required forms.
steps.relevance: Child-related issues commonly shape the procedural path.

steps.title: Review property and finances
steps.description: Assets, debts, and support obligations may affect the process.
steps.relevance: Financial issues can influence agreements and filings.

steps.title: Identify required court forms
steps.description: The appropriate forms can vary depending on the circumstances of the case.
steps.relevance: Form requirements depend on your county and case type.
